name: Passeio Pombalino — circular route (PR3)
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around the Guadiana traverse a diverse landscape featuring wooded hills, dramatic river valleys, and extensive cork oak forests. The Guadiana River is a central feature, carving gorges and converging with other waterways. Hikers can experience varied environments, from steppe lands to unique fields in former mining areas. The region is part of the Guadiana Valley Natural Park, a protected area known for its biodiversity. Best waterfall hiking trails around the Guadiana The most popular waterfall hiking route is El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views within the natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Rugidero – Cornalvo Dam loop from presa romana de Cornalvo, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail features the Cornalvo Dam and surrounding natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Las Muelas Reservoir – El Rugidero loop from El Rugidero, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around the Guadiana is defined by river valleys, cork oak forests, and diverse natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging paths. The routes in Guadiana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Guadiana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on waterfall hikes around the Guadiana?
A: Waterfall hiking trails around the Guadiana traverse a diverse landscape featuring wooded hills, dramatic river valleys, and extensive cork oak forests. The Guadiana River itself is a central feature, carving gorges and converging with other waterways. You can experience varied environments, from steppe lands to unique fields in former mining areas, all within the rich biodiversity of the Guadiana Valley Natural Park.

Q: Are there any famous waterfalls in the Guadiana region?
A: Yes, the Guadiana region is home to the magnificent &lt;strong&gt;Pulo do Lobo Waterfall&lt;/strong&gt; on the Guadiana River, which is described as a breathtaking natural spectacle. While specific routes to this waterfall aren't detailed in this guide, it's a key natural highlight of the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go waterfall hiking in the Guadiana region?
A: The best seasons for hiking in the Guadiana region, including waterfall trails, are generally spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild and pleasant, making it ideal for enjoying the trails and observing the local flora and fauna.

Q: What are some popular hiking routes in the Guadiana region that offer scenic views?
A: One popular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991628272/el-rugidero-circular-by-the-cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 19.8 km trail offering extensive views within the natural park. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6900910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero – Cornalvo Dam loop from presa romana de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.7 km path featuring the Cornalvo Dam and surrounding natural scenery.

Q: Are there any easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners in the Guadiana area?
A: Yes, for those looking for an easier experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir – El Rugidero loop from El Rugidero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.2 km trail that can be completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes, leading through varied terrain. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38141541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir – El Rugidero loop from Parque Natural de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, which is similar in length and difficulty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in the Guadiana region?
A: The routes in Guadiana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys and cork oak forests to the unique natural park environments.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the waterfall hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the Guadiana region boasts several cultural and historical attractions. The historic town of &lt;strong&gt;Mértola&lt;/strong&gt;, perched on the Guadiana River, is known for its Moorish architecture and archaeological sites, including an ancient castle. The old mining town of &lt;strong&gt;Mina de São Domingos&lt;/strong&gt; also offers insight into the region's industrial past.

Q: Can I find places to cool off or swim after a hike in the Guadiana region?
A: Yes, near the old mining town of Mina de São Domingos, you can find &lt;strong&gt;Praia Fluvial de Mina de São Domingos&lt;/strong&gt;, a riverside beach perfect for cooling off in warmer months after your hike.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Guadiana Valley Natural Park?
A: The Guadiana Valley Natural Park is a haven for biodiversity. It's an excellent spot for nature observation, with a chance to spot rare species like the Iberian Midwife Toad and various snakes. Birdwatching is a significant draw, with species such as the black stork, Lesser Kestrel, various eagles, great bustard, little bustard, and black-bellied sandgrouse inhabiting the area.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in the Guadiana region?
A: Yes, many trails in the Guadiana region are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991628272/el-rugidero-circular-by-the-cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6900910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero – Cornalvo Dam loop from presa romana de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; are both popular circular options, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I might encounter on trails around the Guadiana?
A: Beyond the natural features, you might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of Diana (Mérida)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1654225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge over the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant man-made monument in the area.
tips: Praça Marquês de Pombal, the heart of the city. At its center stands an obelisk erected in 1776, from which the radiating mosaic pavement spreads out. The three most important architectural monuments of the 18th century are also located on the square: the church, the town hall and the former guard house.

This beautiful city is not that old! The experience gained in the reconstruction of Lisbon after the earthquake of 1755 was put to practical use in Vila Real de Santo António - first in the careful urban planning, which presented little difficulty due to the flat terrain, and then in the implementation of a strict one architectural order, the checkerboard pattern.

In front of the centre of Vila Real de Santo Antonio we have the marina where numerous recreational boats are moored.
All of them at the mouth of the Guadiana River which forms the border between Spain 🇪🇸 and Portugal 🇵🇹

waypoints.name: Praça Marquês de Pombal, Vila Real de Santo António
Avenida da República, Vila Real de Santo António
